growth_records.cite: Coll et al. Genetic determinants of Staphylococcus aureus adhesion shape virulence trade-offs in bacteremia. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-72657-5. [PMC13279828]
growth_records.snippet: We compared growth and adhesion phenotypes of wild-type USA300 (LAC) or Newman and their glcA mutant strains. The wild-type LAC doubled in 18.86 ( ± 0.6) minutes
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (18.86 min = 0.3143 h) reported directly from exponential-phase fit to microtiter-plate OD600 growth kinetics in TSB at 37°C with aeration. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/0.3143 = 2.205 h^-1.

growth_records.cite: Coll et al. Genetic determinants of Staphylococcus aureus adhesion shape virulence trade-offs in bacteremia. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-72657-5. [PMC13279828]
growth_records.snippet: the glcA mutant displayed a slightly slower doubling time of 21.06 ( ± 1.9) minutes (P = 0.219)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (21.06 min = 0.351 h) reported directly (TSB, 37°C, aerated microtiter-plate culture). growth_rate_per_h = ln2/0.351 = 1.975 h^-1. Difference from WT LAC not statistically significant (P=0.219).

growth_records.cite: Coll et al. Genetic determinants of Staphylococcus aureus adhesion shape virulence trade-offs in bacteremia. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-72657-5. [PMC13279828]
growth_records.snippet: the wild-type Newman doubled in 25.05 ( ± 0.35) minutes
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (25.05 min = 0.4175 h) reported directly (TSB, 37°C, aerated microtiter-plate culture). growth_rate_per_h = ln2/0.4175 = 1.660 h^-1.

growth_records.cite: Coll et al. Genetic determinants of Staphylococcus aureus adhesion shape virulence trade-offs in bacteremia. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-72657-5. [PMC13279828]
growth_records.snippet: the glcA mutant displayed a significantly quicker average doubling time of 23.25 ( ± 1.8) minutes (P = 0.2095)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (23.25 min = 0.3875 h) reported directly (TSB, 37°C, aerated microtiter-plate culture). growth_rate_per_h = ln2/0.3875 = 1.789 h^-1. Note: text calls this change 'significantly quicker' but also reports P=0.2095 (not significant at 0.05); reported as-is from source.

growth_records.cite: Yang et al. Staphylococcus aureus alpha-toxin impairs early neutrophil localization via electrogenic disruption of store-operated calcium entry. Cell Reports (2023). DOI:10.1016/j.celrep.2023.113394. [PMC10731421]
growth_records.snippet: S. aureus has a rapid doubling time in rich media of 20 min.
growth_records.notes: This is a generic background statement in the Discussion (not a primary experimental measurement performed in this immunology-focused study), with no strain, exact medium composition, or culture conditions (temp/pH) specified beyond 'rich media'. Doubling time (20 min = 0.333 h) is stated directly in the paper (growth_method=reported); growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/0.333 h = 2.08 h^-1 was calculated from this stated doubling time. Flagged low confidence because it is not tied to a specific defined medium/strain/experiment within this paper. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, oxygen]

growth_records.cite: Rochat et al. The conserved regulatory RNA RsaE down-regulates the arginine degradation pathway in Staphylococcus aureus. Nucleic Acids Research (2018). DOI:10.1093/nar/gky584. [PMC6158497]
growth_records.snippet: HG003 and HG003 ΔrsaE doubling time are 69 min (±1.1) and 58 min (±1.8), respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time 69 min (=1.15 h) reported directly for HG003 WT grown aerobically (250 rpm, 10:1 flask-to-volume) in CDM lacking glucose (amino acids as sole carbon source). mu = ln2/1.15h = 0.602 h^-1. Temperature not explicitly restated for this specific CDM experiment (general S. aureus culturing elsewhere in paper stated as 37C, not confirmed for this assay). [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]

growth_records.cite: Rochat et al. The conserved regulatory RNA RsaE down-regulates the arginine degradation pathway in Staphylococcus aureus. Nucleic Acids Research (2018). DOI:10.1093/nar/gky584. [PMC6158497]
growth_records.snippet: HG003 and HG003 ΔrsaE doubling time are 69 min (±1.1) and 58 min (±1.8), respectively. ... faster growth rate than HG003 in CDM, which contains amino acids as the sole carb[on source]
growth_records.notes: Doubling time 58 min (=0.967 h) reported directly for HG003 ΔrsaE grown aerobically in CDM lacking glucose (amino acids as sole carbon source). mu = ln2/0.967h = 0.717 h^-1. Glutamate consumption was measured in parallel (Fig 6 right panel) but only presented graphically (ng/µL vs time), no numeric values given in text, so no uptake rate could be extracted. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]

growth_records.cite: Cansizoglu et al. Rapid ultrasensitive detection platform for antimicrobial susceptibility testing. PLoS Biology (2019). DOI:10.1371/journal.pbio.3000291. [PMC6559665]
growth_records.snippet: The doubling times were 29.3 min for P. aeruginosa strain PAO1, 21.1 min for S. aureus RN4220, 20.6 min for E. coli MG1655, and 25.7 min for the E. coli (ET-CI28) isolate.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (21.1 min = 0.3517 h) reported directly from plate-reader OD600 exponential-growth measurements in LB at 37 C with shaking. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2 / doubling_time_h = 0.6931/0.3517 = 1.971 h-1.

growth_records.strain: MRSA ATCC 43300, ptr-inhibited mutant (CRISPR/Cas9 transcriptional inhibition of ptr via pCasiSA)
growth_records.medium: Cation-adjusted Mueller-Hinton (MH) broth, supplemented with 50 mg/L Ca2+
growth_records.cite: Sulaiman et al. Proteomics and Transcriptomics Uncover Key Processes for Elasnin Tolerance in Methicillin-Resistant Staphylococcus aureus. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01393-21. [PMC8788329]
growth_records.snippet: the ptr-inhibited mutant had a much slower growth profile than the evolved strains (with doubling time of ∼110 min) (Fig. 1f).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time of ~110 min (1.833 h) explicitly stated in text (approximate value, 'ptr-inhibited mutant') grown in cation-adjusted MH broth at 37°C with shaking (standard exponential-phase growth conditions used throughout the paper for MRSA ATCC 43300). growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/1.833 = 0.378 h-1. Confidence medium because the value is explicitly approximate (~) and read from a figure reference in text rather than a precise tabulated number.

growth_records.strain: EC139 (ST398 lineage) ΔmecA complemented with plasmid-borne mecA246G allele
growth_records.agit: 200 rpm (routine); continuous shaking for growth-rate assay
growth_records.medium: SSM9PR minimal medium + 16 µg/ml penicillin + 200 ng/ml Atc (inducer)
growth_records.cite: Harrison et al. Genomic identification of cryptic susceptibility to penicillins and β-lactamase inhibitors in methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus. Nature microbiology (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41564-019-0471-0. [PMC7611363]
growth_records.snippet: mecA246G strain grew...with a reduced doubling time (dt) of 5.33 hours (95% CI: 5.28 to 5.38) compared to either mecA246E (dt: 6.65...) or mecA122I (dt: 6.92...)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (dt=5.33 h, 95% CI 5.28-5.38) reported directly in text for strain EC139ΔmecA complemented with mecA246G, grown in SSM9PR minimal medium + 16 µg/ml penicillin (Fig. 6d). Growth rate calculated as mu = ln2/dt = 0.6931/5.33 = 0.1300 h-1. Medium composition (SSM9PR) given in Methods: 1x M9 salts, 2 mM MgSO4, 0.1 mM CaCl2, 1% glucose, 1% casaminoacids, 1 mM Thiamine-HCl, 0.05 mM nicotinamide, 37°C, per ref 51 cited in paper.

growth_records.strain: EC139 (ST398 lineage) ΔmecA complemented with plasmid-borne mecA246E allele ('wildtype'/ancestral)
growth_records.agit: 200 rpm (routine); continuous shaking for growth-rate assay
growth_records.medium: SSM9PR minimal medium + 16 µg/ml penicillin + 200 ng/ml Atc (inducer)
growth_records.cite: Harrison et al. Genomic identification of cryptic susceptibility to penicillins and β-lactamase inhibitors in methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus. Nature microbiology (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41564-019-0471-0. [PMC7611363]
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (dt=6.65 h, 95% CI 6.62-6.69) reported directly in text for strain EC139ΔmecA complemented with 'wildtype' mecA246E, grown in SSM9PR minimal medium + 16 µg/ml penicillin (Fig. 6d). Growth rate calculated as mu = ln2/dt = 0.6931/6.65 = 0.1042 h-1. Same SSM9PR medium composition as reported in Methods.

growth_records.agit: 200 rpm (routine); continuous shaking for growth-rate assay
growth_records.medium: SSM9PR minimal medium + 16 µg/ml penicillin + 200 ng/ml Atc (inducer)
growth_records.cite: Harrison et al. Genomic identification of cryptic susceptibility to penicillins and β-lactamase inhibitors in methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus. Nature microbiology (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41564-019-0471-0. [PMC7611363]
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (dt=6.92 h, 95% CI 6.89-6.96) reported directly in text for strain EC139ΔmecA complemented with mecA122I, grown in SSM9PR minimal medium + 16 µg/ml penicillin (Fig. 6d). Growth rate calculated as mu = ln2/dt = 0.6931/6.92 = 0.1002 h-1. Same SSM9PR medium composition as reported in Methods.

growth_records.cite: O'Brien et al. Decreased efficacy of antimicrobial agents in a polymicrobial environment. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-022-01218-7. [PMC9213441]
growth_records.snippet: dilution rate, D = 8.7 x 10^-2 and 0.102 h^-1 for Q = 145 and 170 uL min^-1, respectively. S. aureus ATCC 25923 (SA) ... grown to a steady-state in ASM under continuous-flow conditions (24 h incubation).
growth_records.notes: Chemostat: V=100 mL, Q=170 uL/min = 10.2 mL/h; D = 0.102 h^-1. At steady state S. aureus net specific growth rate mu = D = 0.102 h^-1; td = ln2/0.102 = 6.80 h. Applies to mono-species SA and polymicrobial (no CA) steady-state cultures. ASM composition by reference [19] only. No uptake/secretion fluxes reported.

growth_records.cite: O'Brien et al. Decreased efficacy of antimicrobial agents in a polymicrobial environment. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-022-01218-7. [PMC9213441]
growth_records.snippet: dilution rate, D = 8.7 x 10^-2 ... for Q = 145 ... For experiments including CA, Q was decreased to 145 uL min^-1.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat: V=100 mL, Q=145 uL/min = 8.7 mL/h; D = 0.087 h^-1. At steady state mu = D = 0.087 h^-1; td = ln2/0.087 = 7.97 h. Applies to S. aureus within polymicrobial PA/SA/CA cultures run at the lower dilution rate (CA/fluconazole experiments). ASM composition by reference [19] only. No uptake/secretion fluxes reported.

growth_records.strain: NE327 [erm(B), erythromycin-resistant; B_E] (JE2/Nebraska transposon library background)
growth_records.cite: Leclerc et al. Growth-Dependent Predation and Generalized Transduction of Antimicrobial Resistance by Bacteriophage. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00135-22. [PMC9040582]
growth_records.snippet: The median estimated maximum growth rates were 1.61 for B_E (95% credible interval, 1.59 to 1.63) ... with a total carrying capacity of 2.76 x 10^9 CFU/mL
growth_records.notes: mu_max reported from logistic-growth model fit to in vitro data = 1.61 /h for B_E (erythromycin-resistant NE327). Units confirmed h^-1 (paper: 'maximum value for mu_max in our model is approximately 1.5' and adsorption rate given in h^-1). Doubling time calculated: td=ln2/mu=0.6931/1.61=0.431 h. Carrying capacity B_max=2.76e9 CFU/mL (2.61e9-2.98e9) reported for total community, not per-strain. No substrate/product fluxes reported (rich BHIB, no metabolite quantification).

growth_records.strain: NE201KT7 [tet(K), tetracycline-resistant; B_T] (JE2/Nebraska transposon library background)
growth_records.cite: Leclerc et al. Growth-Dependent Predation and Generalized Transduction of Antimicrobial Resistance by Bacteriophage. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00135-22. [PMC9040582]
growth_records.snippet: The median estimated maximum growth rates were 1.61 for B_E ..., 1.51 for B_T (1.49 to 1.53), and 1.44 for B_ET (1.42 to 1.47)
growth_records.notes: mu_max reported (logistic fit) = 1.51 /h for B_T (tetracycline-resistant NE201KT7), 95% CI 1.49-1.53. Doubling time calculated: td=ln2/mu=0.6931/1.51=0.459 h. Same medium/conditions as B_E record. No flux data (rich medium).

growth_records.strain: B_ET double-resistant progeny [erm(B)+tet(K)], generated by transduction from NE327 x NE201KT7
growth_records.cite: Leclerc et al. Growth-Dependent Predation and Generalized Transduction of Antimicrobial Resistance by Bacteriophage. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00135-22. [PMC9040582]
growth_records.snippet: ... and 1.44 for B_ET (1.42 to 1.47), with a total carrying capacity of 2.76 x 10^9 CFU/mL (2.61 x 10^9 to 2.98 x 10^9).
growth_records.notes: mu_max reported (logistic fit) = 1.44 /h for double-resistant progeny B_ET, 95% CI 1.42-1.47. Doubling time calculated: td=ln2/mu=0.6931/1.44=0.481 h. B_ET showed no significant fitness cost vs parents (relative fitness 0.96-0.98). No flux data.

growth_records.cite: Busch, Larissa M. et al. Unraveling proteomic chaos by independent component analysis—ClpX proficiency promotes the iron and oxygen limitation responses of Staphylococcus aureus and affects the intracellular bacterial behavior. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01092-25. [PMC12710355]
growth_records.snippet: For the HG001 wild type, the doubling time under control conditions during the exponential growth phase (up to 4 h of cultivation) was ... 34.6 ± 0.5 min ...
growth_records.notes: Proteomics/ICA study; only growth (doubling time) is experimental, no uptake/secretion measured. mu=ln2/td; td=34.6 min=0.5767 h -> mu=0.693147/0.5767=1.202 /h. TSB is a complex undefined medium (composition not stated in paper); AHT added to keep clpX-complement comparable. Aerobic control condition.

growth_records.cite: Busch, Larissa M. et al. Unraveling proteomic chaos by independent component analysis—ClpX proficiency promotes the iron and oxygen limitation responses of Staphylococcus aureus and affects the intracellular bacterial behavior. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01092-25. [PMC12710355]
growth_records.snippet: the doubling time ... was increased from 34.6 ± 0.5 min to 37.6 ± 0.9 min under iron limitation ...
growth_records.notes: mu=ln2/td; td=37.6 min=0.6267 h -> mu=0.693147/0.6267=1.1061 /h. Iron limitation via 600 µM 2,2'-dipyridyl in TSB. No uptake/secretion rates in this proteomics paper.

growth_records.cite: Busch, Larissa M. et al. Unraveling proteomic chaos by independent component analysis—ClpX proficiency promotes the iron and oxygen limitation responses of Staphylococcus aureus and affects the intracellular bacterial behavior. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01092-25. [PMC12710355]
growth_records.snippet: ... and to 43.4 ± 0.3 min under oxygen limitation.
growth_records.notes: mu=ln2/td; td=43.4 min=0.7233 h -> mu=0.693147/0.7233=0.9583 /h. Oxygen limitation via 100% flask-volume fill. Classified microaerophilic (O2-limited batch). No uptake/secretion data.

growth_records.cite: Busch, Larissa M. et al. Unraveling proteomic chaos by independent component analysis—ClpX proficiency promotes the iron and oxygen limitation responses of Staphylococcus aureus and affects the intracellular bacterial behavior. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01092-25. [PMC12710355]
growth_records.snippet: the growth rate of the ΔclpX mutants was reduced ... (doubling time for control: 39.3 ± 0.8 min, iron limitation: 47.9 ± 0.4 min; oxygen limitation: 47.8 ± 1.1 min).
growth_records.notes: mu=ln2/td; td=39.3 min=0.655 h -> mu=0.693147/0.655=1.0582 /h. ΔclpX deletion mutant grows slower than WT under all conditions. No uptake/secretion rates.

growth_records.cite: Busch, Larissa M. et al. Unraveling proteomic chaos by independent component analysis—ClpX proficiency promotes the iron and oxygen limitation responses of Staphylococcus aureus and affects the intracellular bacterial behavior. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01092-25. [PMC12710355]
growth_records.snippet: (doubling time for control: 39.3 ± 0.8 min, iron limitation: 47.9 ± 0.4 min; oxygen limitation: 47.8 ± 1.1 min).
growth_records.notes: mu=ln2/td; td=47.9 min=0.7983 h -> mu=0.693147/0.7983=0.8682 /h. ΔclpX under iron limitation. No uptake/secretion data.

growth_records.cite: Busch, Larissa M. et al. Unraveling proteomic chaos by independent component analysis—ClpX proficiency promotes the iron and oxygen limitation responses of Staphylococcus aureus and affects the intracellular bacterial behavior. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01092-25. [PMC12710355]
growth_records.snippet: (doubling time for control: 39.3 ± 0.8 min, iron limitation: 47.9 ± 0.4 min; oxygen limitation: 47.8 ± 1.1 min).
growth_records.notes: mu=ln2/td; td=47.8 min=0.7967 h -> mu=0.693147/0.7967=0.8701 /h. ΔclpX under oxygen limitation. No uptake/secretion data. Complemented strain (clpXcompl) also cultured but no numeric doubling time reported, so not recorded.

growth_records.cite: Rüger M et al. Species-specific viability analysis of Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Burkholderia cepacia and Staphylococcus aureus in mixed culture by flow cytometry. BMC Microbiology (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2180-14-56. [PMC3995885]
growth_records.snippet: S. a. pure culture mu_max 1.08 ± 0.37 h-1 (Δt 0-4 h). For S. aureus neither gluconate nor 2-KDG were detected (n.d., below detection limit).
growth_records.notes: mu_max=1.08 h-1 reported (Table 1). doubling_time=0.693/1.08=0.642 h. S. aureus consumed glucose/glutamine/glutamate but did NOT produce gluconate/2-KDG (below detection). No numeric uptake rate.

growth_records.cite: Rüger M et al. Species-specific viability analysis of Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Burkholderia cepacia and Staphylococcus aureus in mixed culture by flow cytometry. BMC Microbiology (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2180-14-56. [PMC3995885]
growth_records.snippet: S. a. mixed culture mu_max 1.29 ± 0.31 h-1 (Δt 0-4 h); similar to pure culture. After 12 h cell concentrations decreased slightly in mixed culture.
growth_records.notes: mu_max=1.29 h-1 reported (Table 1, mixed/co-culture). doubling_time=0.693/1.29=0.537 h. Large SD (±0.31). No numeric uptake/secretion rate.
